title: Dev-bridge 'page not connected' error hardcodes localhost:5173 regardless of actual port
summary: engine/dev/agf-dev-bridge.ts:294 has a string-literal `http://localhost:5173` baked into the AGF_BRIDGE_PAGE_NOT_CONNECTED error message. When the Vite dev server runs on any non-default port (e.g. a second project booted via `npm run dev -- --port 5177`, or a CI/playtest worker that auto-picks the next free port), the message points the agent at the wrong URL. The fix is mechanical — `findPage` already receives the incoming request URL, so `url.host` (or `url.origin`) gives the live port. Pre-existing since sprint 32 (commit afafeaf), not a regression — only surfaces when QA runs against a non-default port for parallel debug sessions or when the default port is already taken.
repro: Start the dev server on a non-default port: `npm run dev -- --port 5177` from the repo root.
Do NOT open the project in a browser tab yet (so no page is connected to the bridge).
Observe the response: `{"ok":false,"error":{"code":"AGF_BRIDGE_PAGE_NOT_CONNECTED","message":"No active page on /__agf/ws — open http://localhost:5173 in a tab first."}}`.
Note that the message says `localhost:5173` even though the bridge is bound on port 5177.
expected: The error message points at the same host:port the agent just curled — `open http://localhost:5177 in a tab first.` so the next click is correct.
actual: The error message always says `localhost:5173`, sending the agent (or its operator) to the wrong port. On a fresh machine the user opens 5173 expecting Vite, sees nothing (port unbound), gets stuck.
